machinery_genes.quote: The nucleotide sequence of the DNA fragment encoding these functions was determined and revealed the existence of five open reading frames: aprA, the structural gene encoding APR; aprI, which encodes a protease inhibitor; and aprD, aprE, aprF whose products are involved in protease secretion. The AprD, AprE and AprF proteins share significant homology with proteins implicated in secretion of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin.

and aprD, aprE, aprF whose products are involved in protease secretion. The AprD, AprE and AprF proteins share significant homology with proteins implicated in secretion of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin.

notes: Strain: the apr locus was cloned from a P. aeruginosa PAO1 genomic bank (Guzzo et al. 1990, PMID 2153662), so the AprDEF apparatus is PAO1-native; AprD/AprE/AprF map to PAO1 chromosome AE004091. Three core T1SS components all named: AprD = ABC transporter (inner-membrane ATPase), AprE = membrane fusion protein / HlyD-family adaptor, AprF = TolC-family outer-membrane channel. Family/role assignment per the T1SS brief synonym table and the homology to E. coli HlyB/HlyD/TolC and Erwinia PrtD/E/F noted in Duong 1992; the primary abstract quote names AprD, AprE, AprF together as secretion-apparatus components but does not split the per-gene IM/MFP/OMP assignment in one sentence. The Tommassen 1992 review (PMID 1389315) independently confirms: 'Secretion across the two membranes probably takes place in one step at adhesion zones that may be constituted by three accessory proteins, designated AprD, AprE and AprF.' AprX (Duong et al. 2001, PMID 11179678) is a SUBSTRATE of the Apr T1SS ('a new substrate for the Apr type I secretion system'), co-operonic with aprD/E/F but NOT apparatus. AprA (alkaline protease) is the secreted substrate and AprI is its inhibitor; neither is machinery.
